LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT ALEX PADILLA'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE - DEFENDANT SECRETARY OF STATE DEMURS ON ONE GROUND: "THAT THE ALLEGATIONS FAIL TO STATE SUFFICIENT FACTS TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OF ACTION" - CCP 430.10(E). THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. AT A MINIMUM, A PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS ADEQUATELY PLED. PROCEDURES APPLICABLE TO DEMURRERS IN ORDINARY CIVIL ACTIONS ALSO APPLY TO ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS PROCEEDINGS. (SEE CCP 1109.) THUS, I MAY CONSIDER ONLY THE PLEADING'S CONTENTS, AND ALL OF ITS ALLEGATIONS ARE DEEMED TRUE. (KLEINER V. GARRISON (1947) 82 CAL.APP.2D 442, 445.) THE SECRETARY REQUESTS THAT I ADOPT HIS VIEW OF CONTENTS OF CERTAIN DOCUMENTS, BUT JUDICIAL NOTICE APPLIES ONLY TO FACTS NOT REASONABLY SUBJECT TO DISPUTE. (SEE, E.G., JEFFERSON, CAL. EVIDENCE BENCHBOOK (4TH ED. 2009) JUDICIAL NOTICE, 49.10, P. 1147.) THE SECRETARY IS TO FILE AN ANSWER ON OR BEFORE JULY 6, 2018. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ROBERT BALIAN, CSR #5220 =3022/RU

calendar.matters:
Motion For A Peremptory Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Aug-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Linda Lye, Esq. of American Civil Liberties Union Foundation, 415.293.6313, appearing for the petitioner Asian Americans Advancing Justice;
William S. Freeman, Esq. of American Civil Liberties Union Foundation, 415.621.2493, appearing for the petitioner Asian Americans Advancing Justice;
Raul Luevano Macias, Esq. of American Civil Liberties Union Foundation, 916.824.3265, appearing for the petitioner Asian American Advancing Justice;
Jonathan Stein, Esq. of Asian Americans Advancing Justice;
Anthony P. O'Brien, Esq. of Office of the Attorney General, 916.210.6002, appearing for the respondent Alex Padilla as Secretary of State of California.

Ruling: ARGUED. PETITIONER ASIAN AMERICANS ADVANCING JUSTICE - LOS ANGELES, ASIAN AMERICANS ADVANCING JUSTICE - ASIAN LAW, AMERICAN CIVIL LIBERTIES UNION OF NORTHERN Motion For A Peremptory Writ Of Mandate is taken under submission by the Court. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, Tel. 415.551.5868, Emails: torreanom@aol.com/mtoreano@sftc.org. =(302/RBU)

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Jun-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Linda Lye of the ACLU Legal Dept., Tel. No. 1-415-293-6313 appearing
for the Petitioner;
Atty. David J. Berger of Wilson Sonsini, Tel. No. 1-415-947-2135 appearing for the
Petitioner;
Atty. Anthony P. O'Brien Office of the Atty. General, Tel. No. 1-916-210-6002
appearing for the Respondent.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT ALEX PADILLA'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE - Defendant secretary of state demurs on one ground: "that the allegations fail to state sufficient facts to constitute a cause of action" - CCP 430.10(e). The demurrer is OVERRULED. At a minimum, a petition for writ of mandate is adequately pled. Procedures applicable to demurrers in ordinary civil actions also apply to administrative mandamus proceedings. (See CCP 1109.) Thus, I may consider only the pleading's contents, and all of its allegations are deemed true. (Kleiner v. Garrison (1947) 82 Cal.App.2d 442, 445.) The secretary requests that I adopt his view of contents of certain documents, but judicial notice applies only to facts not reasonably subject to dispute. (See, e.g., Jefferson, Cal. Evidence Benchbook (4th ed. 2009) Judicial Notice, 49.10, p. 1147.) The secretary is to file an answer on or before July 6, 2018. Order signed. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR #5220 =3022/ru
